Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper explicitly states that thermospermine (T-Spm) is synthesized by ACL5 and shows OsACL5 produces T-Spm in vitro and increases T-Spm in planta.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Thermospermine is discussed throughout as one of the major polyamines; thus its class as polyamine is directly supported.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results state PA contents were detected using shoots and leaves of 2-week-old seedlings, and Figure S11c is cited for increased T-Spm contents in OE leaves under high temperature/high light/low humidity.

Source_Species.reason: OsACL5 is the rice ACL5 gene; the study is in rice.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Materials and methods specify rice plants as Oryza sativa cv. Zhonghua 11.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Materials and methods explicitly name Oryza sativa.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The paper states the variety used was Zhonghua 11 (ZH11).

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: All listed effects are reported in Results: OE increases T-Spm, decreases Spd/Spm, reduces ethylene evolution, causes environmentally dependent adaxial leaf rolling, smaller metaxylem vessels, shorter plants, smaller grains, lower 1000-grain weight and reduced yield; mutants increase grain length, 1000-grain weight, and yield per plot.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: qRT-PCR and GUS assays show expression in all tested tissues, especially roots, stems and leaves, with vascular expression, embryos, roots, floral meristem, and mature stem vascular bundles; seeds during grouting are shown in Figure S10.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: This is a paraphrase of the rice expression data: broad expression with especially strong vasculature-associated expression is supported by qRT-PCR and GUS results.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods specify maize ubiquitin promoter for overexpression, native OsACL5 promoter for GUS reporter, and 35S promoter for subcellular localization constructs.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results explicitly show high temperature, high light intensity and low humidity enhance leaf rolling, and Figure S11c is cited for increased T-Spm under those conditions; RNA-seq compared midday versus morning conditions.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are explicitly described in Results/Methods: CRISPR/Cas9, overexpression, in vitro enzyme assay, HPLC, GC ethylene measurement, qRT-PCR, promoter-GUS, subcellular localization, RNA-seq, and haplotype analysis.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Figure 5 and text state OsACL5-eGFP localizes to nucleus, cytoplasm, and plasma membrane.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq was performed using Illumina Novaseq6000 and haplotype analysis used RiceVarMap v2.0; no accession is provided, so 'accession NA' is acceptable as not claiming a specific missing accession.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This summary accurately reflects Results: OsACL5 is the focal gene affecting T-Spm homeostasis, OE raises T-Spm >9-fold and shifts PA/ethylene-related traits, knockout improves grain traits/yield, OE causes environment-dependent leaf rolling linked to metaxylem/water potential, and haplotype analysis suggests conserved T-Spm regulation.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: OsACL5 is identified as a thermospermine synthase/ACL5 family enzyme involved in polyamine biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The Summary states major polyamines play vital roles in growth and adaptation against environmental changes; this supports the stated function at the class/metabolite level.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results explicitly note osacl5 did not significantly reduce T-Spm, and Discussion proposes dynamic balance/back-conversion; the conclusion and summary indicate breeding value for higher yield and imply reducing activity is favorable compared with overexpression.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper states OsACL5 will be an important gene resource for molecular breeding for higher yield, and mutant phenotypes support knockout/reduced activity as beneficial while OE is detrimental.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results document OE phenotypes including shorter plants, thinner culms, shorter panicles, smaller grains, reduced yield, and environmentally sensitive leaf rolling.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Methods for subcellular localization explicitly give the coding sequence identifier Os02g0237100.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: IAA17 and OsRAC3 were used only as nuclear and plasma membrane localization markers in co-localization assays. The paper does not claim they physically interact with OsACL5.

corrections.reason: IAA17 and OsRAC3 were used only as nuclear and plasma membrane localization markers in co-localization assays. The paper does not claim they physically interact with OsACL5.
